hypothesis: If trained with cross-modal predictive objectives that align graph (CGCNN), text (MatSciBERT), and simulated XRD latent spaces using L2 prediction loss, VIC regularization, and contrastive loss, then the resulting model will achieve significantly lower mean absolute error on Matbench crystal property prediction tasks (Band Gap, Shear Modulus, Bulk Modulus, Dielectric, and Perovskite formation energy) and will generalize to AFLOW band gap prediction with minimal finetuning (~0.6% parameters updated) compared to CGCNN, CoGN, and ALIGNN baselines because cross-modal latent alignment produces richer complementary representations.
